mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with trade-offs in complexity and resource requirements depending on the deployment scenario.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.19.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's context.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This configures the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler.
> This sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This will remove the vLLM deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not handled properly.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment vllm-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'vllm-deployment' with your actual deployment name.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. Drain and cordon nodes if necessary, then perform the upgrade and verify the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl set image deployment/vllm-deployment vllm-container=vllm:0.19.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Update the deployment to the new version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running with the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version may not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and version in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient resources or misconfigured node selectors.

**Fix:**
Check resource requests and limits in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-modal LLMs and improved memory management with PagedAttention.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
